reasons: Estes provides constant chain-healing and a powerful AoE ult lock-on, letting Miya stay in fights far longer and out-sustain trades.
His passive movement speed bonus on healed allies helps Miya reposition while kiting.

proof.summary: Estes' Moonlight Immersion healing link and Blessing of the Moon Goddess ultimate pour continuous, high-volume healing onto Miya, so her low base survivability is offset and she can hold her ground attacking from the backline through extended team fights.
worksBestWhen: Miya is positioned behind the frontline within Estes' healing range.
The team fights as a grouped front-to-back unit.
Miya has reached her core item spike so the sustained DPS pays off.
failureCases: Enemy assassins burst Miya faster than heals tick.
Estes gets caught out and dies, removing all sustain.

reasons: Angela's Heartguard ultimate globally attaches to Miya, granting a large shield, healing, and movement speed regardless of map position.
Her Love Waves slow and her Smart Heart passive speed buff let Miya free-hit safely.

proof.summary: Angela's ultimate Heartguard lets her possess Miya from anywhere on the map, layering a shield, heal-over-time, and protection while Miya keeps attacking, turning a fragile hyper-carry into a near-unkillable damage anchor during the possession window.
worksBestWhen: Miya is the team's primary damage dealer worth saving the ult for.
Angela can stay alive at range to channel the global ult.
The enemy commits a dive that the shield absorbs.
failureCases: Heartguard is on cooldown when the dive lands.
Angela is killed before casting the ult.
Heavy burst overwhelms the shield instantly.

reasons: Mathilda's Guiding Wind grants Miya a shield and a linked dash, directly covering Miya's lack of mobility and escape.
Her ult and CC let her engage or peel divers off Miya.

proof.summary: Mathilda's Guiding Wind attaches to Miya, giving her a shield and the ability to dash alongside Mathilda, compensating for Miya's single biggest weakness (no blink or dash) so she can reposition out of danger or chase down kills she otherwise couldn't.
worksBestWhen: Miya needs to escape a dive or reposition in a fight.
Mathilda stays near Miya to keep the link available.
The team plays a mobile, skirmish-heavy tempo.
failureCases: Mathilda dashes away from Miya leaving the link out of range.
Guiding Wind is on cooldown during the engage.
Miya is rooted or suppressed so the dash can't trigger.

reasons: Franco's Iron Hook pulls an enemy directly into Miya's attack range, and Bloody Hunt suppresses them so she can free-hit.
His durability zones assassins away from Miya.

proof.summary: Franco's Iron Hook yanks an isolated enemy into the team, and his Bloody Hunt ultimate locks them in suppression long enough for Miya to dump her full attack-speed DPS into a target that cannot move or fight back, converting a single pick into a guaranteed kill.
worksBestWhen: Franco lands the hook on a high-value squishy target.
Miya is in position to immediately follow up the suppression.
The rest of the enemy team is out of range to retaliate.
failureCases: The hooked target has CC immunity.
Miya is too far away to capitalize before the suppression ends.

reasons: Tigreal's Implosion ultimate group-stuns multiple enemies, giving Miya a window of free, uninterrupted damage on a clustered team.
His frontline body, plus the knock-up from Sacred Hammer's second cast, shield Miya from divers.

proof.summary: Tigreal engages with Sacred Hammer into Implosion, dragging and stunning the enemy team into a tight cluster; Miya follows up with Moon Arrow's extra arrows and Arrow of Eclipse to spread damage across multiple immobilized targets, turning his initiation into a team wipe.
worksBestWhen: Tigreal catches multiple enemies in the ult radius.
Miya hangs back ready to follow up immediately after the stun lands.
The team groups for an objective fight where the AoE pays off.
failureCases: Tigreal flickers in and dies before Miya can follow up.
Enemies are spread out so Implosion only grabs one.
Miya gets focused by a diver who ignores the frontline.
